---
title: "DFOS Metadata Model"
sidebar: intro
---

This document outlines a data model and API for practical management of distributed fiber-optic sensing(DFOS) metadata in the context of [DASDAE](https://dasdae.org). The purpose is to demonstrate:

- The model's conceptual advantages,
- An ergonomic API that integrates with DASCore, and 
- Solicit feedback from the community.

The goal is not necessarily to [proliferate a new standard](https://xkcd.com/927/), but to make an elegant metadata solution in the DASDAE context.   

::: {.proposal-note}
This site is a proposal. Code snippets are illustrative sketches and are not expected to run against the current DASCore implementation. 
:::

## Why not X? 

Managing DFOS metadata is a significant challenge, and the are many different approaches. Before introducing the DASDAE model, we must examine these approaches and highlight what is lacking. 

### Adhoc Archives

 In many cases, ad-hoc efforts mix time-series with loosely related metadata: field notes, acquisition spreadsheets, cable diagrams, tap tests, OTDR traces, maps, and post-deployment corrections, etc. This is particularly common for university-lead research projects. The following shows an example of what this might look like.

```text
field_campaign/
├── das/
│   ├── 2024-05-01_000000.h5
│   ├── 2024-05-01_010000.h5
│   └── 2024-05-01_020000.h5
├── notes/
│   ├── field_notebook_shawn.png
│   ├── tap_tests.xlsx
│   └── deployment_log.md
├── geometry/
│   ├── map.kml
│   ├── tap_test.csv
│   └── channel_locations_corrected.csv
├── instrument/
│   ├── interrogator_headers.json
│   └── acquisition_settings.xlsx
└── corrections/
    ├── bad_channels.csv
    ├── timing_offset_notes.txt
    └── distance_pick_updates.csv
```

However, as the experiment progresses often different versions/revisions of the metadta appear, more data is collected (perhaps with different interrogators or settings), and more researcher work on the dataset, each generating and interpreting the existing metadata, the process becomes hard to manage and bug-prone.   


### PRODML
In some industries, DFOS metadata has been integrated into broader domain standards, such as Energistics [PRODML](https://energistics.org/prodml-developers-users). PRODML works well within an oil and gas production context, including DAS/DTS use cases, but it does not fit naturally as a general-purpose format for non-oil/gas experiments common in seismology. For example:

  - PRODML is primarily an upstream oil/gas production data-transfer standard, not a standalone DFOS or seismology standard.
  - Its DAS/DTS components are embedded in a larger model around production reporting, wells, pipelines, facilities, flow tests, PVT/fluid analysis, product volumes, and operations.
  - PRODML DAS assumes an Energistics stack: XML data objects, EPC packaging, HDF5 external arrays, data-object references, facility/wellbore/pipeline-oriented metadata.
  - That is useful for oilfield DAS exchange, but heavy and domain-specific for general seismological experiments, academic DFOS surveys, dark fiber monitoring, glaciers, volcanoes, urban arrays, etc.

### StationXML

Seismologists quickly discovered that trying to cram all useful metadata/information into every file produced by a DAS interrogator creates bloated files which become data archiving nightmares, particularly when metadata revisions require modifying many produced files. 

The solution is to create a "sidecar data" model. The time series files store a minimal amount of information, then point to the external metadata model which can be more comprehensive and easier to revise. This solution is already widely deployed by the seismology community ([FDSN](https://www.fdsn.org/)) in the [StationXML](https://www.fdsn.org/xml/station/) standard. 

Station XML defines a hierarchy of objects:

- Inventory: a collection of metadata
- Network: a collection of stations with unique identifying information
- Station: roughly, a geographical monitoring intention (includes lat/lon/elevation) and a collection of channels
- Channel: Specific time-series information, including the instrument type/name, response information, etc.

::: {.render_data_model spec="specs/stationxml.yml"}
:::


### FDSN DAS Metadata Standard

The recent [FDSN DAS Metadata Standard](https://docs.fdsn.org/projects/das-metadata/en/draft/) outlined by @lai2024toward demonstrates one workable solution, and is currently being used/considered by FDSN providers for DAS archival. Its draft schema is organized around a parent overview block with two main branches: the instrument/acquisition description and the sensor-location description.

::: {.render_data_model spec="specs/fdsn-das-metadata.yml"}
:::

This is a strong archival model, but it is not identical to the problem DASCore needs to solve. DASCore needs metadata that can be incomplete, revised, attached to local analysis patches, and projected onto coordinates such as channel, index, optical distance, or physical geometry without requiring every archive to become submission-grade at the start.

## DASDAE Inventory

::: {.signal-grid}
::: {.signal}
### Sidecar-first

Store metadata beside raw or processed files instead of forcing every file format to carry the full description.
:::

::: {.signal}
### Patch-aware

Use `data_source_id` to resolve the acquisition and fiber-array context for a patch when enrichment is needed.
:::

::: {.signal}
### Fiber-native

Represent optical distance, geometry, coupling, components, and annotations as first-class path metadata.
:::
:::

::: {.render_data_model spec="specs/dasdae-inventory.yml"}
:::

The important separation is intentional: the inventory records relatively durable context, while `PatchAttrs` records patch-local state such as processing history, tags, units after processing, and the identifier used to resolve inventory context.

## Proposal Sections

- [Model At A Glance](intro/model.qmd) explains the object graph and identity model.
- [Standards Context](intro/standards.qmd) maps the proposal to StationXML, FDSN DAS Metadata, and PRODML.
- [Scenarios](scenarios/index.qmd) shows how the model supports sidecar metadata, enrichment, geometry, coupling, and revisions.
- [API](api/index.qmd) sketches the conceptual API and manifest shape.
